Leah's Eyelashes Falling Out from Crying for a Tzaddik
The Reason God Opened Leah's Womb: She Hated Eisav's Ways

macro_segments.title: The Counting Discrepancy: Yocheved's Birth in Egypt and the Seventy Souls

micro_segments.title: The Discrepancy Between the Total (Seventy) and the Sum of Named Individuals (Sixty-Nine)
The Internal Problem with Leah's Children Count
The Proposed Solution: Dina Had a Twin Sister
The Resolution: Yocheved Was Born During the Journey to Egypt

macro_segments.title: Yaakov's Recognition of Yosef's Indispensability: Why Yaakov Left Lavan Once Yosef Was Born

Proof Text: The House of Yaakov as Fire, House of Yosef as Flame, House of Eisav as Stubble
Challenge: King David Defeated Amalek Without Yosef's Descendants
Response: David Also Had Help from Menashe (Yosef's Tribe)

Resolution: Yish'ei, Though Listed Under Shimon, Was Actually a Descendant of Menashe

macro_segments.title: The Bechor's Double Portion in Various Categories of Inherited Property and Gifts

micro_segments.title: The Five Categories Where the Bechor Gets Double: Zroa, Lechayim, Keiva, Mukdashin, and Shevach

macro_segments.title: The Bechor's Double Portion in the Gifts of the Kohen: Mechari Kehunah and the Principle of Meshuchres

micro_segments.title: The Question: How Can the Bechor Get Double in Gifts That Arrive After the Father's Death?
The Clarification: The Case Must Involve Mechari Kehunah (Established Kohen Relationships)
The Principle: The Gifts Are Considered as if Already Separated and in the Kohen's Possession
The Supporting Idea: Gifts of a Kohen Are Considered as if Already Separated Before Being Given

macro_segments.title: The Bechor's Double Portion in Kodashim Kalim (Lesser Holy Things): The View of Rabbi Yosi HaGelili

micro_segments.title: The Problem: How Can the Bechor Get Double in Sacrificial Animals That Are Not the Father's Property?
The Solution: The Case Involves Shelamim (Peace Offerings), Which Are Considered the Owner's Property
Rabbi Yosi HaGelili's Principle: Shelamim Are Considered the Owner's Property Even While Sanctified
Textual Support for Rabbi Yosi HaGelili's View: The Verse on Trespass Against God
The Implication: Even Non-Kohanim Who Sanctify Shelamim Give the Bechor a Double Portion of the Meat
